name: TA-65 metabolic syndrome trial reported cardiometabolic biomarker movement
summary: In a small randomized crossover metabolic-syndrome trial matched to NCT02531334, TA-65 was associated with favorable movement in HDL-C, BMI, waist circumference, LDL/HDL ratio, and TNF-alpha, but not broad aging, functional, disease-event, or survival endpoints.
statement: In a 40-person metabolic-syndrome randomized crossover trial registered as NCT02531334, TA-65 was associated with higher HDL-C and lower BMI, waist circumference, LDL/HDL ratio, and TNF-alpha versus placebo, but the result remains disease-context biomarker evidence rather than broad aging, functional, or survival evidence.
population_or_model: Adults aged 32 to 70 years with metabolic syndrome
time_horizon: Two 12-week treatment periods separated by a 3-week washout
quantitative_note: Abstract reports p < 0.05 for HDL-C, BMI, waist circumference, LDL/HDL ratio, and TNF-alpha changes; HDL-C changes correlated with CRP (r = -0.511, p < 0.01), ALT (r = -0.61, p < 0.001), and TNF-alpha (r = -0.550, p < 0.001).
caveats: Metabolic-syndrome cardiometabolic biomarkers are disease-context surrogate endpoints, not LEV-relevant functional, disease-event, or survival outcomes.
ClinicalTrials.gov's primary outcome was plasma insulin; the abstract's positive result emphasis is on HDL-C, anthropometrics, LDL/HDL ratio, TNF-alpha, and correlated inflammation markers.
The publication does not resolve long-term telomerase activation safety or cancer-risk uncertainty.
The ClinicalTrials.gov registry still lacks a posted results table, so registry-level transparency remains incomplete.
